# sysf.io — full profile

> sysf.io is a Swiss sovereign-AI engineering studio. We fine-tune Apertus — Switzerland's fully open LLM from EPFL, ETH Zurich and CSCS — on a client's own documents and run it inside their infrastructure: on-premise, Swiss cloud, or fully air-gapped. Open weights the client's auditors can inspect, no US API in the loop. Clients own the resulting model and receive a documented handover.

## Positioning

AI for the data you can't send away. For regulated Swiss and EU organisations, external AI isn't
forbidden — it's slow and residual-risk-heavy: months of legal review, DPAs, sub-processor chains,
and at the end a model the organisation still doesn't control. Most projects die at the pilot
stage. Switzerland answered with Apertus, a fully open, sovereign, multilingual model (open
weights, documented training data) from EPFL, ETH Zurich and the Swiss National Supercomputing
Centre (CSCS), trained on the Alps supercomputer in Lugano. sysf.io turns that open foundation
into working systems that clear compliance review.

The approach is proven in production: the Canton of Ticino runs a fine-tuned Apertus-8B
translation service on its own cantonal infrastructure, reaching 94% accuracy after fine-tuning.

## Six pillars

1. Swiss by foundation — built on Apertus, Switzerland's sovereign open LLM (open weights, documented training data).
2. Compliant by design — built for Swiss FADP/nFADP and EU GDPR, with documentation supporting EU AI Act obligations; transparency and auditability from the architecture.
3. Sovereign & private — data never leaves the client's jurisdiction; on-prem, Swiss cloud or confidential compute; no US API dependency; zero data egress.
4. Customised on your data — domain fine-tuning plus RAG and agentic tools on the client's corpus, systems and terminology.
5. End-to-end delivery — data → fine-tune → evaluate → deploy → monitor → maintain. A maintained production system, not a proof-of-concept.
6. Multilingual, incl. Swiss — native German, French, Italian and Romansh, including Swiss German.

## Ownership — built to be fired

Everything sysf.io builds belongs to the client: the fine-tuned weights, the data pipelines, the
evaluation harness and the operational runbooks. A documented handover is a deliverable in every
engagement — the client can run the system without sysf.io, or hand it to any team they choose.
No rented model, no lock-in.

## The platform pipeline (seven steps, one jurisdiction)

1. Discovery & compliance scoping — map use case, data sources and regulatory frame (FADP, GDPR, EU AI Act); define success metrics and guardrails.
2. Private, in-jurisdiction data prep — curate and structure data where it lives; design PII handling in; no external services.
3. Fine-tuning on Apertus — teach domain, terminology, formats and tasks; validated against a retrieval-only baseline; full transparency over weights and data lineage.
4. Agent & RAG orchestration — agents, retrieval-augmented generation and tool calls into the client's systems.
5. Evaluation & guardrails — use-case-specific eval set, safety and PII guardrails, output constraints; quality reported against agreed metrics before go-live.
6. Sovereign deployment — containerised deployment to Swiss cloud, on-premise hardware, or confidential-compute VMs (AMD SEV-SNP / Intel TDX with remote attestation).
7. Monitoring & iteration — continuous monitoring of quality, drift and usage; maintenance cadence under a support agreement.

## Technical stack

Fine-tuned Apertus at the core; LangChain and LangGraph for agent orchestration; RAG over client
knowledge; tool calling into client systems; evaluation harnesses and guardrails; container-based
deployment to Swiss cloud, on-prem, or confidential compute. RAG first; fine-tuning where it earns
its keep — every Pilot includes an ablation on the client's evaluation set (base model vs. RAG vs.
fine-tune vs. both). Model-agnostic by design: Apertus, Llama or Mistral — whichever wins on the
evaluation set. No dependency on external US LLM APIs.

Hardware: a quantized, fine-tuned Apertus-8B runs at interactive speed on a single
workstation-class GPU server (roughly 24–48 GB VRAM); the 70B needs a multi-GPU node or a
confidential-compute instance. Pilots can run on hardware sysf.io brings.

## Industries and sample use cases

- Banking & Finance (CID, Art. 47 banking secrecy, FINMA outsourcing due diligence): advisory copilots; KYC/AML document processing; regulatory and policy Q&A (DE/FR/IT/EN); research/reporting assistants. On-prem or confidential compute, zero data egress, audit rights and exit plan documented.
- Healthcare & Pharma (Art. 5 nFADP / Art. 9 GDPR, cantonal data-protection law, KIS integration via HL7/FHIR): discharge letters and clinical reports from structured notes (DE/FR/IT); clinical coding support (CHOP/ICD-10); research and literature assistants; administrative automation. Deliberately outside MDR/MepV scope — no diagnostic or triage functionality.
- Legal & Professional Services: contract analysis and clause extraction; multilingual legal research; drafting grounded in the firm's templates and precedent; due-diligence and data-room summarisation. Privilege-safe, in-jurisdiction.
- Government & Public Sector: multilingual citizen-service assistants (DE/FR/IT/RM); internal knowledge assistants; document/application processing; plain-language drafting. Proven playbook — the Canton of Ticino's fine-tuned Apertus-8B runs on its own infrastructure at 94% accuracy.
- Insurance (FINMA outsourcing-ready): claims intake and document automation against core systems (Guidewire, Syrius, SAP FS-CM); policy and coverage Q&A copilots; underwriting research; correspondence drafting. Traceable, grounded, on-prem option.
- Manufacturing & Industrial: maintenance/troubleshooting copilots; knowledge assistants over manuals/SOPs/specs; quality and compliance documentation; supplier/procurement processing. On-prem/edge-friendly; protects operational IP.

## Compliance

Compliance is engineered into the architecture: in-jurisdiction processing and data lineage for
Swiss FADP/nFADP; data minimisation, purpose limitation and residency for EU GDPR; documented
training data, transparency and guardrails supporting EU AI Act obligations. Compliance and audit
documentation is delivered with the system. This is not legal advice; sysf.io works with the
client's compliance and legal teams.

## Engagement model and pricing

Land-and-expand across three stages. Every tier is sovereign — the client's data stays in their
jurisdiction; tiers differ in deployment depth. Pricing is scoped per engagement, delivered as a
fixed-scope proposal before commitment — no open-ended time-and-materials.

- Pilot — prove value on one use case: private data prep, fine-tuning and retrieval on Apertus, evaluation report vs. agreed metrics (with ablation), production roadmap. Runs in the client's environment or a dedicated single-tenant Swiss cloud under a DPA; at project end all data and derived weights are deleted or handed over — the client's choice.
- Production — the pilot hardened for daily use: everything in Pilot plus agents, RAG and tools wired to the client's systems, guardrails, Swiss-cloud or on-prem deployment, monitoring and evaluation, support and maintenance agreement.
- Air-gapped — regulated / most sensitive environments: everything in Production plus fully on-prem/confidential compute, multiple models and agents, dedicated operations, complete compliance and audit documentation, enterprise support commitments.
- Add-ons: compliance & audit pack (model card, training-data lineage record, DPA template, TOMs, EU AI Act risk-classification worksheet, evaluation report), additional languages, additional use cases, managed hosting.

Clients own the resulting model, because sysf.io builds on the open Apertus model and fine-tunes
inside the client's environment. A documented handover is a deliverable in every engagement.

## Three ways to get sovereign AI — honest comparison

- sysf.io (studio): data on the client's infrastructure; open weights fine-tuned on the client's corpus; sysf.io builds the product against an agreed eval plan; accuracy measured before go-live; client owns the model, handover is a deliverable; compliance documentation delivered with the system.
- Sovereign platform (hosted endpoint): data in the provider's Swiss datacentre; shared base model; the client's team builds on the endpoint; subscription access ends with the contract; platform-level compliance only. Right answer for organisations with their own AI engineering team that want infrastructure only.
- US cloud API (Swiss region): closed weights under US legal reach; the client's team builds on the API; rented access; shared-responsibility compliance. Right answer when the data can leave.

sysf.io is the answer when the data can't leave and the client wants the system built, measured
and handed over.

## Contact

- Email: hello@sysf.io
- Website: https://sysf.io
- Based in Switzerland; serving Swiss and EU organisations.
